id: chunk_Quick_Pdf-data_Appointment of Anti-Ragging Squad (ARS) for Upcoming Semester.md_1
text:
### 2. Preliminary Inquiry into Ragging Complaints

The Anti-Ragging Squad (ARS) shall conduct an on-the-spot preliminary inquiry into any ragging incident referred by:

* Dean (Student Affairs)
* Parents of concerned students
* Students reporting such incidents

### 3. Collection of Evidence and Reporting

The Squad is empowered to:

* Collect incident reports
* Gather supporting documents
* Record facts and viewpoints related to the incident

The collected material shall be submitted to:

* Dean (Student Affairs)
* Anti-Ragging Committee (ARC)

for further inquiry and final action.

The Committee may also take immediate action under the Institute's Anti-Ragging Policy, subject to intimation and approval from the Dean (Student Affairs) / Anti-Ragging Committee.

### 4. Scheduled Surprise Visits

ARS members shall conduct surprise visits across the campus during:

**09:00 PM – 12:00 Midnight**

on their respective assigned duty dates.

### 5. Coordination with Institute Authorities

Th

id: chunk_Quick_Pdf-data_Some Points to Remember About Ragging.md_0
text:
# Some Points to Remember About Ragging

## 1. What Constitutes Ragging?

Ragging is an act of aggression committed by an individual or a group of individuals against another individual or group. The aggressors derive authority merely from being senior to the victims, while the victims become targets simply because they are new to the institution.

> Any interaction that is **aggressive**, **intimidating**, or **asymmetric in power dynamics** (not on equal footing) constitutes ragging.

---

## 2. Ragging Is Not Harmless Fun

In an institution such as IIT Jammu, students come from diverse social, cultural, linguistic, and economic backgrounds. An act that may appear harmless or humorous to one person can cause humiliation, distress, or psychological harm to another.

There have been numerous incidents across the country where ragging has resulted in:

* Severe emotional trauma
* Loss of self-esteem
* Depression and anxiety
* Suicides
* Fatalities

Often, senior students do not realize

metadata.doc: Quick/Pdf-data/Some Points to Remember About Ragging.md
metadata.title: Some Points To Remember About Ragging

id: chunk_Quick_Pdf-data_Some Points to Remember About Ragging.md_1
text:
## 6. Freshers Deserve the Freedom of Self-Discovery

A premier institution like IIT Jammu offers numerous opportunities for students to excel in:

* Academics
* Research
* Innovation
* Sports
* Cultural activities
* Leadership roles
* Entrepreneurship

Imposing predetermined beliefs, behaviors, or expectations on freshers restricts their ability to discover their own interests, strengths, and identities.

---

## 7. Ragging Harms the Reputation of the Nation

It is a matter of concern that searches for the term **"ragging"** frequently highlight incidents from India.

In many countries, ragging has largely disappeared from educational institutions without reducing:

* Institutional pride
* Student bonding
* Alumni engagement
* Sense of belonging

Healthy interaction and mentorship can thrive without ragging.

---

## 8. Eliminating Ragging Is a Collective Responsibility

Ragging cannot be eradicated solely through strict rules, disciplinary measures, or punishments.

Long-term change

metadata.doc: Quick/Pdf-data/Some Points to Remember About Ragging.md
metadata.title: Some Points To Remember About Ragging

# Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

**Jagti, NH-44, Nagrota, Jammu (Jammu & Kashmir) – 181221**
**Email:** registrar@iitjammu.ac.in

---

**Notification No.: 42 of 2026**
**Reference No.: IIT Jmu/Reg./2026/93**
**Date:** 15 June 2026

# Student Grievance Redressal Committee (SGRC)

## References

1. DO No. F.1-13/2022 (CPP-II) dated 19.09.2024 and IITJMU/SW/11-17/2/2024/930 dated 24 September 2024.
2. IITJMU/SW/11-17/2/2025/286 dated 13 October 2025.

---

## Constitution of the Student Grievance Redressal Committee

In compliance with the Gazette Notification dated **11 April 2023**, and superseding earlier notifications issued under the references cited above, the Competent Authority is pleased to constitute the **Student Grievance Redressal Committee (SGRC)** at IIT Jammu as follows:

| S. No. | Name / Designation            | Role            | Remarks                      |
| ------ | ----------------------------- | --------------- | ---------------------------- |
| 1      | De

## Issued By

**(Col. Virinder Singh Jeji (Retd.))**
**Registrar**
Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

---

## Distribution

### Copy To

1. Director's Office
2. Webmaster (for uploading on the Institute website)

---

## Purpose of the SGRC

The Student Grievance Redressal Committee (SGRC) serves as the formal institutional mechanism for addressing and resolving grievances raised by students concerning academic and non-academic matters. The committee functions in accordance with UGC regulations and Institute policies to ensure fairness, transparency, and timely redressal of student concerns.

id: chunk_Quick_Pdf-data_Zero-Tolerance for Ragging in the Institute.md_1
text:
### Subject: Zero-Tolerance for Ragging in the Institute

Dear Parents,

As you know, with the upcoming academic year, IIT Jammu will be welcoming a fresh batch of students who have qualified the JEE (Advanced). Being an Institute of National Importance, IIT Jammu is pleased to bring in this new batch of bright and talented students.

Every year, IIT Jammu puts in great effort to ensure that the new entrants are well adjusted to their new environment. This effort is put in by the faculty and students alike by organising events such as the Foundation Program, Cultural Fest, student-led orientations, and many other extracurricular and co-curricular activities which ensure that the students develop a sense of self-confidence and team spirit.

As you may be well aware, the senior batches play a vital role in creating a welcoming and amicable environment for the new batch. We are sure that your ward, who is one of the senior students, will put in the best of efforts to play a constructive r

## Introduction

The Insitute Ethics Committee of IIT-Jammu is an independent committee under the Dean, Research and Consultancy. The IEC, IIT-Jammu, is responsible for the scrutiny and approval of all research projects in IIT-Jammu involving human subjects. It is responsible for ensuring that ethical principles are followed in the collection of experimental data, and the analysis of such data. It will also ensure the maintenance of security and confidentiality of subject data, and in all ways ensuring that the r

## Constitution

The consitution of the IEC, IIT-Jammu follows the guidelines given by the Indian Council for Medical Research (ICMR). The IEC, IIT-Jammu contains several external experts as well as experts from the faculty members of IIT-Jammu.

It acts as an internal mechanism for redressal and plays a vital role in sensitizing employees about their rights and educating them about the prevention of sexual harassment.
